preview_text:
The model favors Uzbekistan at 47%, while DR Congo's win probability sits at 23%. The underdog case for DR Congo faces a meaningful deficit in the statistical forecast, though draws occur in nearly a third of modeled outcomes.

The expected result distribution suggests a competitive but unbalanced match. Uzbekistan holds a clear edge, but the 30% draw probability indicates the teams are close enough that a stalemate remains the second-most-likely outcome. DR Congo's path to victory is narrower than a draw, implying the model sees this as asymmetric competition rather than a mismatch.

The market and model diverge sharply on this fixture. Sportsbooks assign DR Congo a 41% win chance—nearly double the model's 23%—and price Uzbekistan at only 30%, some 17 percentage points below the statistical forecast. Polymarket consensus aligns closely with the books. The dashboard makes no claim to adjudicate between these sources; the gap reflects substantive disagreement on which team is more likely to prevail.
